rulings.Case Title: MENESES, DIONISIO E. et al vs. DOMINGUEZ, MARY JEAN et al

rulings.Case Title: MONICA ANN ARRAMBIDE VS. GAY-STRAIGHT ALLIANCE NETWORK et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 8, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT GAY-STRAIGHT ALLIANCE NETWORK MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendant shifts the burden as to each cause of action and plaintiff fails to raise any triable issues of material fact. No opposition raised as to 5th cause of action for breach of contract and 9th cause of action breach of implied covenant, and this employment was at-will. As to 1st cause of action for violation of L.C. section 970, no evidence of any knowingly false representations, especially given the brief, one-month time priod of employment. Second cause of action for wrongful termination fails due to lack of public policy basis per L.C. 970 and FEHA. As to 3rd cause of action for promissory fraud, no evidence that Laub had no intention of performing on what she and plaintifff had discussed. Regarding 4th cause of action for negligent misrepresentation, statements do not amount to representations as to a material fact and no evidence Laub did not actually believe what she conveyed to plaintiff. As to the 6th cause of action for promissory estoppel, claimed misrepresentations do not constitute "clear promises," and one month time period not sufficient to allow for performance. Regarding 7th cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, no outrageous conduct shown. Eighth cause of action for negligent infliction fails due to basis of claim being intentional acts. As to 10th cause of action for race discrimination and 11th cause of action for failure to prevent race discrimination, no evidence that race discrimination occurred. Defendant presented evidence of legitimate, nondiscriminatory factors for the adverse employment action and plaintiff fails to raise evidence that the reasons were untrue or pretextual. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue To Orange County (Ccp 395(A) And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1990 Against Pltf And Her Attorney, Elizabeth Karnazes, Esq., Purusantto C.C.P 396(B); Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Motion To Change Venue To Orange
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 8, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT LAWRENCE MOY, LAW OFFICES OF LAWRENCE A.MOY,cMotion To Change Venue To Orange County (CCP 395(A) and Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1990 Against Plaintiff and Her Attorney, Elizabeth Karnazes, Esq., Pursuant to C.C.P 396(B). rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL JOSEPH SMITH VS. UC BOARD OF REGENTS, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 8, 2010, line 5, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL SMITH Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. This motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is denied, the proposed causes of action are cognizable. Also, Plaintiff may not name the individuals on the tortious discharge cause of action because such a claim may only be brought against an employer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH BANDIERA VS. DUNCAN EASTWOOD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 8, 2010, line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT JOSEPH BANDIERA MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, as moot in view of ruling on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH BANDIERA VS. DUNCAN EASTWOOD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 8, 2010, line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT JOSEPH BANDIERA DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to first and second causes of action, no opposition. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to third and fourth causes of action. Cross-complainant to plead damages as a result of the harm, specifying damages on the fiduciary duty claim, and that cross-defendant took property that was rightly cross-complainant's on the conversion claim. Demurrer overruled as to the fifth cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JUANA MANZANERES VS. CESAR GONZALES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Juana Manzaneres Order To Show Cause Re: Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Of 01/21/10)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 8, 2010, line 10, Plaintiff Juana Manzaneres Order to Show Cause Re: Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Of 01/21/10). Motion granted in part, no opposition filed. Plaintiff to prepre new proposed order deleting #1 and #2 as those requests merely order defendants to obey the law and are unnecessary. Motion granted as to request #3 only; however, the "certain enumerated repairs and corrections" must be set forth in detail. =(302/CWW)
